title: Two Voices on the Same Budget
subtitle: A shared activity for hearing how your partner narrates the financial picture differently from how you do
excerpt: A structured activity for couples: you and your partner look at the same financial document and take turns narrating what you see, without interrupting or correcting. The differences in the two narrations are the starting point.

practice.whenToUse: Use when money disagreements in a partnership feel like a clash of values rather than a clash of numbers — when the same facts are being read very differently.

content.text: A shared activity for two people who look at the same financial picture and see different things. One person narrates. The other listens without correcting. Then they switch. The differences in the two narrations are the data — the place the real conversation can start.
New to activities as practice? Read
Sit together with one shared financial document — the monthly budget, the bank account summary, a savings statement. Choose something real, not hypothetical.
One person speaks first. Narrate what you see — what you notice, what feels good, what feels tight, what the numbers mean to you. The other person listens without responding.
Then switch. The other person narrates what they see, without correcting the first narration.
Afterwards, name the differences in the two narrations. Not who was right. What each person was responding to. That is the conversation.
